title: TC-ONB-001: Validate consumer product name (not code name) on onboarding device discovery screen
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20) to ensure it is in pairing mode.
Ensure Poly Studio Mobile app is not installed or has been freshly installed (clear app data).
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app for the first time (first-launch onboarding).
Observe the device discovery/scanning screen that lists available devices.
Verify the device name displayed in the discovered devices list.
Tap the device to proceed to pairing.
expected: The device is displayed using its consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') — NOT an internal code name like 'Falcon'. The name matches the official product branding.

title: TC-ONB-002: Validate consumer product name on onboarding pairing/connection confirmation screen
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20) to ensure it is in pairing mode.
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed through onboarding until the device discovery screen.
Tap the discovered device (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') to initiate pairing.
On the pairing/connection confirmation screen (shown during Bluetooth pairing handshake), observe the device name displayed.
Complete the pairing process.
expected: The pairing/connection confirmation screen displays the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') — NOT an internal code name like 'Falcon'. The name is consistent with the device discovery screen.

title: TC-ONB-003: Validate consumer product name on onboarding success/completion screen
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20) to ensure it is in pairing mode.
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed through the full onboarding flow: device discovery → pairing → connection confirmation.
On the final onboarding success/completion screen (e.g., 'Your device is ready' or similar), observe the device name displayed.
Complete onboarding and enter the main app screen.
expected: The onboarding success/completion screen displays the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') — NOT an internal code name like 'Falcon'. The name is consistent across all onboarding screens.

title: TC-ONB-004: Validate consumer product name on onboarding screens after factory-reset-triggered re-onboarding
steps: Ensure a Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20) is already paired and connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Perform a factory reset on the Poly device (hold specific button combination per device manual to reset).
Observe the app behavior — the app should detect the device is no longer connected and may trigger a re-onboarding or re-pairing flow.
If the app shows a re-onboarding/discovery flow, observe the device name on the discovery screen.
Proceed through re-pairing and observe the device name on the pairing confirmation screen.
Complete the re-pairing process.
expected: After factory reset triggers a re-onboarding flow, all screens (discovery, pairing, completion) display the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') — NOT an internal code name like 'Falcon'.

title: TC-ONB-005: Validate consumer product name on onboarding screens for multiple device types (asymmetry check)
steps: Factory reset Poly Sync 20 speakerphone.
Factory reset a second compatible device (e.g., Poly Sync 20+ or a Poly headset like Poly Voyager 4320).
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and observe the device discovery screen — both devices should appear in the list.
Verify the displayed name for each device.
Tap the first device (e.g., Poly Sync 20) and observe the pairing confirmation screen name.
Complete pairing, then go back and repeat the onboarding flow for the second device (clear app data again or use 'Add Device' flow if available).
Observe the device name for the second device on discovery and pairing screens.
expected: Each device type displays its own correct consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20' for Sync 20, 'Poly Sync 20+' for Sync 20+, 'Poly Voyager 4320' for the headset). No device shows an internal code name like 'Falcon'.

title: TC-ONB-006: Validate consumer product name persists correctly after app backgrounding during onboarding
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20).
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed to the device discovery screen on onboarding.
Verify the device name shown is the consumer product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20').
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds, then reopen the app from the recent apps list.
Observe the device name on the discovery screen.
Tap the device and proceed to the pairing confirmation screen.
Press the Home button again to background the app.
Reopen the app and observe the device name on the pairing confirmation screen.
expected: After app backgrounding and reopening during onboarding, the device name remains the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') on all screens — NOT an internal code name like 'Falcon'.

title: TC-ONB-007: Validate consumer product name after app force-kill during onboarding
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20).
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed to the device discovery screen on onboarding.
Verify the device name shown is the consumer product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20').
Force-kill the app (swipe from recent apps or use 'Force Stop' in Settings).
Re-launch the app.
Observe the device name on the discovery screen (app may restart onboarding or resume from a saved state).
If the app resumes to the pairing confirmation screen, verify the device name there as well.
expected: After force-kill and relaunch during onboarding, the device name remains the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') — NOT an internal code name like 'Falcon'.

title: TC-ONB-008: Validate consumer product name on onboarding screens with localization (English vs. non-English locale)
steps: Set the phone system language to English (US).
Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20).
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed through onboarding — observe device name on discovery, pairing, and completion screens.
Note the exact device name string displayed.
Change the phone system language to a non-English language (e.g., French, German, Japanese).
Clear app data again / fresh install the app.
Launch the app and proceed through onboarding again.
Observe the device name on discovery, pairing, and completion screens.
expected: The device name displayed is the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') in both English and non-English locales. The product name itself should NOT be translated or replaced with a code name. Localized string resources (e.g., button labels, instructions) may change, but the device name remains the correct product name.

title: TC-ONB-009: Validate that device name on onboarding matches device name on main app screen after pairing
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20).
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed through the full onboarding flow.
On the device discovery screen, note the device name displayed.
On the pairing confirmation screen, note the device name displayed.
On the onboarding completion screen, note the device name displayed.
Complete onboarding and enter the main app home screen.
Observe the device name shown on the main device card or device tile (e.g., under 'My Devices' or the top banner).
expected: The device name displayed on all onboarding screens (discovery, pairing, completion) matches the device name displayed on the main app home screen after pairing. All instances show the consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20'), not an internal code name.

title: TC-ONB-010: Validate that onboarding screens do not display internal code name in any UI element (title, subtitle, description)
steps: Factory reset the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20).
Clear app data / fresh install Poly Studio Mobile app.
Launch the app and proceed through the full onboarding flow.
On the device discovery screen, inspect ALL text elements: title, subtitle, device list item name, any description text.
On the pairing confirmation screen, inspect ALL text elements: title, subtitle, device name, any instructional text.
On the onboarding completion screen, inspect ALL text elements: title, subtitle, device name, any congratulatory text.
Take screenshots of each screen for documentation.
expected: No UI element on any onboarding screen contains the internal code name (e.g., 'Falcon'). All references to the device use the correct consumer-facing product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20'). The code name does not appear in titles, subtitles, descriptions, or any visible text.
